strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\nExecutes fetch commands concurrently, streams partial results, and tracks\ncoverage of which sources succeeded/failed.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, AsyncIterator, Callable, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom SourceResult,\n)\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# Cluster proxy mappings from CLAUDE.md\nKUBECTL_PROXIES = {\n    \"apexalgo-iad\": \"http://traefik-apexalgo-iad:8001\",\n    \"ardenone-cluster\": \"http://traefik-ardenone-cluster:8001\",\n    \"ardenone-hub\": \"http://traefik-ardenone-hub:8001\",\n    \"ardenone-manager\": \"http://traefik-ardenone-manager:8001\",\n \"iad-options\": \"http://traefik-iad-options:8001\",\n    \"iad-ci\": \"http://traefik-iad-ci:8001\",\n}\n\nARGOCD_RO_PROXY = \"https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444\"\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP:            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS: self._fetch_bead_details,\n            FetchSource.CI_STATUS: self._fetch_ci_status,\n            FetchSource.COMPONENTS: self._fetch_components,\n            FetchSource.LOGS: self._fetch_logs,\n            FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: self._fetch_topic_context,\n            FetchSource.REMINDERS: self._fetch_reminders,\n        }\n\n    async def fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n\n                if on_partial_result:\n                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n                sources[source] = result\n\n                if result.status == \"success\":\n                    succeeded.append(source)\n                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                    timed_out.append(source)\n                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n                elif result.status == \"error\":\n                    failed.append(source)\n                    if required:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                    else:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # Try to derive from project_slug\n            if context.project_slug:\n                namespace = context.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": \"No namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name = pod.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\")\n                    phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n                        c.get(\"restartCount\", 0)\n                        for c in pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n                    )\n\n                    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\n _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project.\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path or not _fetch_kubectl_status(self, project_slug: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch general kubectl status for a project.\"\"\"\n        cluster = self._project_clusters.get(project_slug)\n        if not cluster:\n            return {\"error\": f\"No cluster mapping for project: {project_slug}\"}\n\n        proxy = self._kubectl_proxies.get(cluster)\n        if not proxy:\n            return {\"error\": f\"No proxy for cluster: {cluster}\"}\n\n        # Try to get namespace from project_slug\n        namespace = project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n\n        # Use httpx to query kubectl proxy\n        import httpx\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                # Get pods in namespace\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                # Extract relevant info\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name = pod.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\")\n                    phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n                        c.get(\"restartCount\", 0)\n                        for c in pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n                    )\n\n                    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n                return {\n                    \"cluster\": cluster,\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                # Namespace doesn't exist\n                return {\n                    \"cluster\": cluster,\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"error\": \"Namespace not found\",\n                    \"pods\": [],\n                }\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\n                \"cluster\": cluster,\n                \"namespace\": namespace,\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"pods\": [],\n            }\n\n    async def _fetch_pod_status(self, project_slug: str, args: list[str]) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch detailed pod       if not os.path.exists(repo_path):\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path does not exist: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Run git log with author and date\n            proc =          # Parse br output (JSON format)\n                try:\n                    beads = json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                    return {\n                        \"project\": FileNotFoundError:\n            return {\n                \"project\": project_slug,\n                \"error\": \"br CLI not found\",\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n status (Argo Workflows) for a project.\"\"\"\n        # This would query Argo Workflows on iad-ci\n        import httpx\n\n        try:\n            # Use kubectl proxy to query workflows\n            proxy = self._kubectl_proxies.get(\"iad-ci\")\n            if not proxy:\n                return {\"error\": \"No proxy for iad-ci cluster\"}\n\n            # Get recent workflows for this project\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/argo-workflows/workflows\",\n                    params={\"labelSelector\": f\"project={project_slug}\"}\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                workflows_data = resp.json()\n\n                workflows = []\n                for wf in workflows_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    workflows.append({\n                        \"name\": wf.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\"),\n                        \"phase\": wf.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\"),\n                        \"started_at\": wf.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n                        \"finished_at\": wf.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"finishedAt\", \"\"),\n                        \"message\": wf.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"message\", \"\"),\n                    })\n\n                # Sort by started_at desc\n                workflows.sort(key=lambda x: x.get(\"started_at\", \"\"), reverse=True)\n\n                return {\n                    \"project\": project_slug,\n                    \"workflows\": workflows[:10],  # Last 10\n                    \"count\": len(workflows),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\n                \"project\": project_slug,\n                \"error\": str(e),\n            }\n\n    def _get_pod_ready(self, pod_data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get pod ready status (e.g., '1/1').\"\"\"\n        container_statuses = pod_data.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n        if not container_statuses:\n            return \"0/0\"\n\n        ready_count = sum(1 for cs in container_statuses if cs.get(\"ready\", False))\n        total_count = len(container_statuses)\n\n        return f\"{ready_count}/{total_count}\"\n\n\n# Global fetch executor instance\n_executor: def success_rate(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate success rate (0.0 to 1.0).\"\"\"\n        if self.total_sources == 0:\n            return 0.0\n        return len(self.succeeded) /     return self.sources.get(source)\n\n    def get_successful_data(self) -> dict[FetchSource, dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get data from all successful sources.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            source: prompt\n57\tROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n58\t\n59\t## Your Role\n60\t\n61\tGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n62\t1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n63\t2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n64\t3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n65\t4. **Assign** urgency tier\n66\t\n67\t## Output Format\n68\t\n69\tReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n70\t\n71\t```json\n72\t[\n73\t  {\n74\t    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n75\t    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n76\t    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n77\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n78\t    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n79\t    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n80\t  }\n81\t]\n82\t```\n83\t\n84\t## Intent Types\n85\t\n86\t- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n87\t- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n88\t- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n89\t- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n90\t- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n91\t- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n92\t- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n93\t- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n94\t\n95\t## Task-Profile Classification\n96\t\n97\tRoute to **task-profile** when:\n98\t- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n99\t- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n100\t- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n101\t- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n102\t- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n103\t- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n104\t\n105\tTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n106\t\n107\t## Urgency Tiers\n108\t\n109\t- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n110\t- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n111\t- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n112\t- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n113\t\n114\t## Routing Logic\n115\t\n116\tUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n117\t- Look for direct project name matches\n118\t- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n119\t- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n120\t- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n121\t\n122\t## Segmentation Guidelines\n123\t\n124\t- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n125\t- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n126\t- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n127\t\n128\t## Confidence Threshold\n129\t\n130\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n131\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n132\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n133\t\n134\tReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n135\t\n136\t\n137\tclass IntentRouter:\n138\t    \"\"\"\n139\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n140\t\n141\t    For task-profile      utterance: str,\n164\t        session_id: str,\n165\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n166\t        \"\"\"\n167\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n168\t\n169\t        Uses LLM to {session_id}\")\n198\t\n199\t        try:\n200\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n201\t                system_prompt=ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,\n202\t       logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n221\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n222\t\n223\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n224\t \"\"\"\n258\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n259\t\n260\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n261\t\n262\t        Args:\n263\t        intent_id=intent_id,\n280\t                classification=classification,\n281\t                session_id=session_id,\n282\t                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n283\t            )\n284\t            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n285\t\n286\t        return routed_intents\n287\t\n288\t    async def process_intent(\n289\t        self,\n290\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n291\t    ) -> dict:\n292\t        \"\"\"\n293\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n294\t\n295\t        Args:\n296\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n297\t\n298\t        Returns:\n299\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n300\t        \"\"\"\n301\t        classification = RoutedIntent,\n319\t    ) -> dict:\n320\t        \"\"\"\n321\t        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLE bead.\n322\t\n323\t        Args:\n324\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to         \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n340\t                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n341\t            },\n342\t            metadata={\n343\t                \"urgency\":       result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n351\t\n352\t            return {\n353\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n354\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n355\t                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n356\t                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n357\t                \"pending_card\": logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n363\t            return {\n364\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n365\t Router - classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\nUses LLM to classify intents by type and project, then routes:\n- task-profile intents → escalate strand (bead creation)\n- other intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..escalate.handler import EscalateRequest, escalate_intent\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n\n\n# Router system prompt\nROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Urgency Tiers\n\n- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            import json\n\n            intents_data = json.loads(response)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n            return await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent)\n\n        # For other intents, return placeholder for now\n        # TODO: Integrate with fetch + synthesize strands\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"message\": f\"Intent routed to {classification.intent_type.value} strand (not yet implemented)\",\n        }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLE bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n\n\ndef get_router(store=None) -> IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global intent router instance.\"\"\"\n    global _router\n    if _router is None:\n        _router = IntentRouter(store=store)\n    return _router\n","numLines":382,"startLine":1,"totalLines":382}}}

Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--metadata\", json.dumps(metadata),\n                stdin=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            # br output format: \"Created bead abc-123\"\n        # or just the bead ID\n        lines = output.strip().split(\"\\n\")\n        for line in lines:\n            if \"Created bead\" in line or \"bead\" in line.lower():\n                # Try to extract ID (format: abc-123)\n                parts = line.split()\n                for part in parts:\n                    if \"-\" in part and len(part) > 5:\n                        return part\n\n        # Partial results are passed to the Synthesize strand as they arrive.\n\nTimeout per source: 5 seconds\n\n## Result Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"pods\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* pod objects */ ],\n    \"cached_at\": null\n  },\n  \"argocd\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": { /* application state */ },\n    \"cached_at\": \"timestamp if cache was used\"\n  },\n  \"git\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": {\n      \"commits\": [ /* commit objects */ ],\n      \"branch\": \"current-branch\",\n      \"uncommitted\": [ /* changed files */ ]\n    }\n  },\n  \"beads\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* bead objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"pods\": true,\n    \"argocd\": false,\n    \"git\": true,\n    \"beads\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Fallback Behavior\n\n- **Source timeout**: Mark as timeout, continue with other sources\n- **All sources timeout**: Return error with caveat that infrastructure may be unreachable\n- **Partial success**: Return what we have with coverage fields\n\n## Context Expansion\n\nFor status queries, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **Cluster context**: Which cluster this project runs on\n- **Namespace context**: Which namespace\n- **Repo path**: Local path to git repo\n- **Project slug**: For bead lookup\n\nThe fetch layer is deterministic. No LLM calls here — just execute the command matrix and return structured data.\n","numLines":107,"startLine":1,"totalLines":107}}}

Create a task bead with:\n23\t# br create --type action --project ${PROJECT_SLUG} \"${ACTION_COMMAND}\"\n24\t```\n25\t\n26\t### Git Actions\n27\t\n28\t```bash\n29\t# Current state\n30\tgit -C ${REPO_PATH} status\n31\tgit -C ${REPO_PATH} log -1 --oneline\n32\t\n33\t# Action (via NEEDLE task bead)\n34\t# Never execute git commands directly. Create bead for approval workflow.\n35\t```\n36\t\n37\t### Deployment Actions\n38\t\n39\t```bash\n40\t# Check current deploy state\n41\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get deployment --kubeconfig=${KUBECONFIG} get workflows -n argo-workflows -l app=${APP_NAME} -o json\n46\t```\n47\t\n48\t## Safety Model\n49\t\n50\t**All mutable actions are routed through NEEDLE task beads**, never executed directly by the fetch strand.\n51\t\n52\tThis ensures:\n53\t- Audit trail (bead captures who/what/when)\n54\t- Approval workflow (bead closes only after user confirmation)\n55\t- Reversibility (bead body includes rollback instructions)\n56\t\n57\t## Action Classification\n58\t\n59\t### Auto-Approve Actions (Low Risk)\n60\t\n61\tThese can be auto-approved via exception rules:\n62\t- `kubectl logs` (read-only)\n63\t- `kubectl describe` (read-only)\n64\t- Git status checks (read-only)\n65\t\n66\t### Manual Approval Required (High Risk)\n67\t\n68\tThese always go through bead approval workflow:\n69\t- `kubectl delete`\n70\t- `kubectl apply` with production changes\n71\t- Git push to main\n72\t- Deployment promotion\n73\t\n74\t## Result Structure\n75\t\n76\t```json\n77\t{\n78\t  \"pre_state\": {\n79\t    \"pods\": [ /* current pod state */ ],\n80\t    \"deployment\": { /* current deployment spec */ ],\n81\t    \"argocd\": { /* current sync state */ ]\n82\t  },\n83\t  \"action_spec\": {\n84\t    \"type\": \"kubectl|git|workflow\",\n85\t    \"command\": \"the command to execute\",\n86\t    \"risk_level\": \"low|medium|high\",\n87\t    \"rollback\": \"how to undo this\"\n88\t  },\n89\t  \"impact_analysis\": {\n90\t    \"affected_resources\": [ /* what will change */ ],\n91\t    \"side_effects\": [ /* what else is impacted */ ],\n92\t    \"dependencies\": [ /* other systems that care */ ]\n93\t  },\n94\t  \"approval_required\": true\n95\t}\n96\t```\n97\t\n98\t## Escalation to Task Bead\n99\t\n100\tFor actions requiring approval, the fetch strand returns a result that prompts escalation:\n101\t\n102\t```json\n103\t{\n104\t  \"escalate\": {\n105\t    \"to_bead\": true,\n106\t    \"bead_spec\": {\n107\t      \"type\": \"action\",\n108\t      \"project\": PROJECT_SLUG,\n109\t      \"command\": ACTION_COMMAND,\n110\t      \"preconditions\": PRE_STATE,\n111\t      \"risk_level\": RISK_LEVEL\n112\t    }\n113\t  }\n114\t}\n115\t```\n116\t\n117\tThe escalate strand creates the bead and returns a pending card.\n118\t\n119\t## Context Expansion\n120\t\n121\tFor action queries, include:\n122\t- **User identity**: Who is requesting this action\n123\t- **Project context**: Which project/environment\n124\t- **Risk assessment**: Based on action type and target Create a task bead with:\n# br create --type action --project ${PROJECT_SLUG} \"${ACTION_COMMAND}\"\n```\n\n### Git Actions\n\n```bash\n# Current state\ngit -C ${REPO_PATH} status\ngit -C ${REPO_PATH} log -1 --oneline\n\n# Action (via NEEDLE task bead)\n# Never execute git commands directly. Create bead for approval workflow.\n```\n\n### Deployment Actions\n\n```bash\n# Check current deploy state\nkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get deployment ${DEPLOYMENT} -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\ncurl -s https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444/api/v1/applications/${APP_NAME}\n\n# CI workflow status\nkubectl --kubeconfig=${KUBECONFIG} get workflows -n argo-workflows -l app=${APP_NAME} -o json\n```\n\n## Safety Model\n\n**All mutable actions are routed through NEEDLE task beads**, never executed directly by the fetch strand.\n\nThis ensures:\n- Audit trail (bead captures who/what/when)\n- Approval workflow (bead closes only after user confirmation)\n- Reversibility (bead body includes rollback instructions)\n\n## Action Classification\n\n### Auto-Approve Actions (Low Risk)\n\nThese can be auto-approved via exception rules:\n- `kubectl logs` (read-only)\n- `kubectl describe` (read-only)\n- Git status checks (read-only)\n\n### Manual Approval Required (High Risk)\n\nThese always go through bead approval workflow:\n- `kubectl delete`\n- `kubectl apply` with production changes\n- Git push to main\n- Deployment promotion\n\n## Result Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"pre_state\": {\n    \"pods\": [ /* current pod state */ ],\n    \"deployment\": { /* current deployment spec */ ],\n    \"argocd\": { /* current sync state */ ]\n  },\n  \"action_spec\": {\n    \"type\": \"kubectl|git|workflow\",\n    \"command\": \"the command to execute\",\n    \"risk_level\": \"low|medium|high\",\n    \"rollback\": \"how to undo this\"\n  },\n  \"impact_analysis\": {\n    \"affected_resources\": [ /* what will change */ ],\n    \"side_effects\": [ /* what else is impacted */ ],\n    \"dependencies\": [ /* other systems that care */ ]\n  },\n  \"approval_required\": true\n}\n```\n\n## Escalation to Task Bead\n\nFor actions requiring approval, the fetch strand returns a result that prompts escalation:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"escalate\": {\n    \"to_bead\": true,\n    \"bead_spec\": {\n      \"type\": \"action\",\n      \"project\": PROJECT_SLUG,\n      \"command\": ACTION_COMMAND,\n      \"preconditions\": PRE_STATE,\n      \"risk_level\": RISK_LEVEL\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThe escalate strand creates the bead and returns a pending card.\n\n## Context Expansion\n\nFor action queries, include:\n- **User identity**: Who is requesting this action\n- **Project context**: Which project/environment\n- **Risk assessment**: Based on action type and target environment\n\nActions are irreversible. The fetch layer's job is to capture all context needed for safe approval.\n","numLines":127,"startLine":1,"totalLines":127}}}

Logs may take longer; stream them if possible.\n\nTimeout per source:\n- Logs: 10 seconds (can be slow for large pods)\n- Events: 5 seconds\n- Pod status: 5 seconds\n\n## Result Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"logs\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": {\n      \"pod\": \"pod-name\",\n      \"logs\": \"log output here\",\n      \"line_count\": 100\n    }\n  },\n  \"events\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* event objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"pods\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* pod objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"logs\": true,\n    \"events\": true,\n    \"pods\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Streaming Support\n\nFor logs, prefer streaming the response line-by-line as they arrive from kubectl.\nThis makes the lookup feel faster even when logs are large.\n\n## Context Expansion\n\nFor lookup queries, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **Pod context**: Which pod, namespace, container\n- **Time context**: Recent timestamp from user query (\"last hour\", \"since 2pm\")\n- **Filter context**: Error-only, warnings, specific keywords\n\nThe fetch layer is deterministic. No LLM calls here — just execute the command matrix and return structured data.\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

\"canvas\"\n682\t):\n683\t    \"\"\"\n684\t    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n685\t\n686\t    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n687\t    \"\"\"\n688\t    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n689\t\n690\t    # Get or create session\n691\t    store = await get_store()\n692\t    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n693\t    if not    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n703\t\n704\t    # Create SSE connection\n705\t    connection = broadcaster.register(\n706\t        surface_id=surface_id,\n707\t        session_id=session_id,\n708\t        surface_type=surface_type,\n709\t    )\n710\t\n711\t    # Send workload summary on connect\n712\t    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n713\t\n714\t    async def event_stream():\n715\t        try:\n716\t            # Send initial connection event\n717\t            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n718\t                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n719\t                \"session_id\": session_id,\n720\t            })\n721\t\n722\t            yield _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n727\t                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n728\t                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n729\t                })\n730\t\n731\t            # Stream events\n732\t            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n733\t                yield event_data\n734\t\n735\t        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n736\t            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n737\t            raise\n738\t\n739\t    return StreamingResponse(\n740\t        event_stream(),\n741\t        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n742\t        headers={\n743\t            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n744\t            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n745\t            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",\n746\t        },\n747\t    )\n748\t\n749\t\n750\t# Feedback and Self-Modification endpoints\n751\t@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\n752\tasync def Intent Router System Prompt\n\nYou are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Complex multi-step work that requires durable async handling via NEEDLE bead\n\n## Urgency Tiers\n\n- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse the project registry to map utterances to projects:\n\n1. Look for direct project name matches\n2. Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n3. Use context from previous utterances in the session\n4. If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\nThe user will provide the utterance.
